name: python-control (Control Systems Analysis and Design)

performance.notes: NumPy/SciPy-based; fast for typical control system sizes

agent_readiness.notes: MATLAB Control Toolbox equivalent for Python. import control. sys = control.tf([1], [1, 2, 1]). bode: control.bode_plot(sys). nyquist: control.nyquist_plot(sys). step: T, yout = control.step_response(sys). poles = control.poles(sys). zeros = control.zeros(sys). State space: control.ss(A, B, C, D). Feedback: control.feedback(sys, 1). PID: control.pid(Kp, Ki, Kd). LQR: K, S, E = control.lqr(sys, Q, R). Kalman filter. Discretize: control.sample_system(sys, Ts). Simulink-free control design.

gotchas: No type annotations — poor IDE support
Some functions have MATLAB-style outputs (multiple return values as tuples)
Nonlinear systems require scipy.integrate — not built-in
Robust control (H-infinity) requires slycot optional dependency
best_when: Control systems analysis (Bode, Nyquist, root locus, step response)
Teaching control theory without MATLAB license
avoid_when: Nonlinear model predictive control (use do-mpc or CasADi)
summary: python-control provides MATLAB Control Toolbox equivalent for Python. control.tf([1], [1, 2, 1]). bode_plot(), nyquist_plot(). step_response(). poles(), zeros(). control.ss(A, B, C, D). LQR, Kalman filter. Feedback loops. Discretize. BSD-3 by Richard Murray/Caltech. Standard for control systems analysis in Python. No type annotations. Nonlinear systems require scipy. H-infinity needs slycot.
